summary: E2E testing of MediTrack Special Ordination refactoring. Fixed critical bug (Pill icon not imported). All special ordination features working correctly: time-of-day selection in popup, hiding normal form fields when ordination is active, summary card display, and save without day-doses validation.

issues: HTML warning: <span> inside <option> element. React hydration warning but functionality works.

passed_tests: TEST 1: User Registration - Register new user with PIN 1234, Name Test User, Email test@test.com
TEST 2: Add Medicine - Created Panodil 500mg with stock 30, reminder 7 days
TEST 3: Navigate to Schedule page and click 'Tilføj' (Add) button
TEST 5: Special ordination button appears directly below the medicine dropdown (16px gap)
TEST 6: Click Special ordination button - popup opens correctly
TEST 7: In popup: Time-of-day selector with 4 options (Morgen 08:00, Middag 12:00, Aften 18:00, Nat 22:00) visible
TEST 8: In popup: Select time of day, set start date, choose repeat pattern, and click Save
TEST 9: After saving ordination: Time slot dropdown is HIDDEN and day dose selector (Mon-Sun) is HIDDEN
TEST 10: Ordination summary card shows: selected time of day, dates and repeat pattern
TEST 11: Save button is enabled (even with no day-doses selected)
TEST 12: Click Save to create schedule entry with special ordination - CREATED SUCCESSFULLY
TEST 13: Normal flow test: Without special ordination, time slot dropdown and day doses appear normally
Backend API: 28/29 tests passed (96.5%) - Special ordination CRUD endpoints working correctly

bugs_fixed.issue: ReferenceError: Pill is not defined - Missing import from lucide-react

action_items: MINOR: Fix HTML validation warning - <span> inside <option> element in Schedule.js select elements
critical_code_review_comments: Special ordination feature implemented correctly with all requirements met
Time-of-day selection (Morgen, Middag, Aften, Nat) properly integrated into popup
Form fields correctly hidden when ordination is active
Save button validation allows saving with special ordination without day_doses
Backend properly stores and returns special_ordination data

success_rate.backend: 96.5% (28/29 tests - 1 cleanup test failed due to test order)

seed_data_creation: Multiple test users created during testing. Test data isolated per run using timestamp-based emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Special ordination feature fully tested. Key findings: 1) Special ordination button is correctly positioned 16px below medicine dropdown. 2) Popup contains time-of-day selector with 4 time slots. 3) When ordination is saved, normal time slot dropdown and day dose selector are hidden. 4) Ordination summary shows time of day (e.g., 'Vælg tidspunkt på dagen: Morgen (08:00)'), start date, and repeat pattern. 5) Fixed critical bug - Pill icon import missing from Medicines.js.
rca_of_issues: Critical Bug: Medicines.js component was using <Pill> icon but it wasn't imported from lucide-react. Fix: Added 'Pill' to the import statement on line 7-18.
